final_message:
All five functions are implemented and pass every docstring example plus the edge cases the specs call out. Summary of what each does:

- **`parse_duration`** — a single `re.fullmatch` with optional ordered `h`/`m`/`s` groups. `fullmatch` naturally rejects out-of-order, duplicate, numberless, negative, whitespace, and trailing-garbage inputs; a leading guard handles empty/non-string.
- **`luhn_valid`** — strips spaces/hyphens, rejects empty or non-ASCII-digit strings (returning `False`, never raising, even for non-strings and unicode digits like `²`), then runs the standard right-to-left doubling checksum.
- **`flatten`** — recursive walk building a fresh dict; empty nested dicts contribute nothing, non-dict values (including lists) are kept, and the input is never mutated.
- **`top_k_frequent`** — `Counter` + sort by `(-count, word)` for the frequency-desc / alphabetical-asc ordering; returns `[]` for `k <= 0` and clamps naturally when `k` exceeds distinct-word count.
- **`rolling_max`** — O(n) monotonic-decreasing deque of indices; raises `ValueError` for `window <= 0` and returns `[]` when `window > len(nums)`. Verified against `max()` on a 200k-element input.

Uses only the standard library (`re`, `collections`), and signatures/docstrings are unchanged.
